items.title: MoF unveils support measures for MSMEs amid global energy crisis
items.summary: The initiatives include a RM5 billion allocation under the Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP), an extended transition period for e-invoicing implementation and interim exemptions on import duties and sales tax for re-imported Malaysian goods. "SJPP will also collaborate with banks to facilitate loan restructuring and rescheduling, as well as targeted repayment assistance," it said. Meanwhile, the MoF said the government is also extending the transition period for e-invoicing implementation.

items.title: Atomic Energy department finds no radiation compliance issues after investigation into UMMC’s alleged ‘cancer cluster’
items.summary: DENGKIL, 20 April 2026 – The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (MOSTI), through the Atomic Energy Department, has responded to claims of a cancer cluster at the Nuclear Medicine Unit of the University Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM). Under the Atomic Energy Act 1984 (Act 304) and the Atomic Energy Licensing (Amendment) Act 2025 (A1779), the licensing authority for radioactive materials, nuclear materials and radiation generators for non-medical purposes is the Director General of the Atomic Energy Department (Atom Malaysia). For medical purposes, the licensing authority is the Director General of Health, Ministry of Health Malaysia (KKM).

items.title: 'We were shocked,' says father of man shot by suspected cattle thieves
items.summary: Abdul Kadir Mohamad, 73, said he was at home with his 64-year-old wife, Siti Zabedah Besar and their son Amirul Akil when they received news about the attack involving his eldest son, Mohamad Khairi Amri, 42. The former Felda settler said a friend called about 7am informing them that Khairi had been shot and was being treated at Tanah Merah Hospital. "A friend called and told us to go there… he said my son had been shot and was in hospital.

items.title: Antarctic ozone healing, but climate change poses new threat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian-led scientific study has confirmed that the ozone layer above Antarctica is gradually recovering, but warns that climate change is introducing new risks that could affect its long-term stability. The research, led by Justin Sentian of Universiti Malaysia Sabah, was published this year in the journal Atmospheric Research. Analysing 19 years of Nasa satellite data, the international research team found that ozone levels over Antarctica are increasing at a measurable rate – offering fresh evidence that global efforts under the Montreal Protocol are working.

items.title: Gentari steps up push for 30-40 GW renewable capacity by 2030
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global clean energy company Gentari is strengthening efforts to achieve its target of 30 to 40 gigawatts (GW) of renewable energy (RE) capacity by 2030, underpinned by rapid growth but contingent on market readiness and returns. Gentari chief strategy and portfolio officer M Huzaini Ghazali said the company remained committed to the target, although delivery would depend on both execution capability and external conditions. Gentari's growth momentum has been supported by improving policy and market conditions, particularly as governments and corporations accelerate the shift towards cleaner energy amid rising conventional fuel costs.

items.title: RM18bil flows into JS-SEZ as Johor doubles down on growth push
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor has stepped up its bid to anchor Southeast Asia's next growth corridor, with more than RM18 billion in foreign direct investments (FDI) already channelled into the Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) since 2024. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the JS-SEZ had moved beyond concept to execution, drawing "real investments, real connectivity and real transformation" on the back of deepening integration with Singapore. Speaking at the Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone Strategic Forum 2026, co-organised by United Overseas Bank (UOB) and Invest Johor, he said growth momentum was being driven by policy clarity, public-private alignment and sustained investor engagement.

items.title: Zahid denies claim of state polls delay over 'fear of defeat'
items.summary: DUNGUN: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has dismissed claim by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin that the unity government plans to delay state elections in Melaka and Johor out of fear of defeat. Zahid, who is also Umno president, said the former prime minister should not assume the current administration would act in the same way as the government did under his leadership. Zahid stressed that there is no question of postponing the state polls in Melaka and Johor as the dissolution of state legislative assemblies rests with the respective rulers.

items.title: Johor-Singapore SEZ enters delivery phase as Johor tailors ecosystem for investors
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor is sharpening its pitch to global investors by repositioning the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) as a fully integrated, demand-driven investment platform, with customised talent pipelines and shared infrastructure emerging as the centrepiece of its next growth phase. "We are no longer just attracting investors; we are configuring the ecosystem around them," he said, adding that Johor is tailoring labour and skills deployment through the Johor Talent Development Council (JTDC) to meet precise industry needs. Hasni said investor concerns are increasingly centred on operational certainty, particularly around utilities, talent availability and long-term sustainability commitments.

items.title: Prosecution seeks to drop the word ‘received’ from Lim Guan Eng’s undersea tunnel corruption charge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — The prosecution has applied to amend the first charge against former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng in the RM6.3 billion major roads and undersea tunnel corruption case. Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin informed Sessions Court Judge Azura Alwi that the amendment involves dropping the word “received” from the charge. Under the first charge, Lim, 65, is accused of abusing his position as Penang chief minister to receive RM3.3 million in return for helping a company owned by businessman Zarul Ahmad secure the Penang Major Roads and Tunnel Construction Project, valued at approximately RM6.34 billion.

items.title: Work on Perak elephant sanctuary to kick off this year
items.summary: IPOH: Construction of Phase One of the Perak Elephant Sanctuary in Hulu Perak is scheduled to begin this year, marking a key step in the state government's efforts to strengthen wildlife conservation and biodiversity management. State Science, Environment and Green Technology Committee chairman Teh Kok Lim said the project, approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan, will be implemented in phases, with the first phase focusing on core infrastructure development. He said the land had been gazetted following approval by the state executive council, reflecting the state's commitment to wildlife conservation and sustainable, conservation-based ecotourism.

items.title: Kedah MB urges assemblymen to safeguard integrity, fight corruption
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor called on state assemblymen in Kedah to play a firmer role as guardians of integrity and a check-and-balance mechanism, to ensure governance remains transparent, accountable and free from corruption. "The role of an assemblyman is not merely to convey the people's aspirations, but to ensure every policy is understood, implemented effectively, and monitored transparently at the grassroots level," he said at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) engagement programme with Kedah Assemblymen 2026, Wisma Darul Aman today. In light of this, he reminded that every government allocation must be managed with integrity, every project implemented transparently, and every decision made without conflict of interest.

items.title: Foodcourt shut, area subdued after triple shooting
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Shops were closed, so too were several business premises along Jalan Mangga Dua in Taman Kota Jaya yesterday which was the scene of a triple murder on Sunday afternoon. There were no customers when the suspect arrived. Otherwise, the child might have witnessed the shooting or even been harmed," the witness said.

items.title: Court of Appeal dismisses Indira Gandhi’s bid to adduce fresh evidence in lawsuit against IGP, Home Ministry and govt
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 20 — The Court of Appeal has dismissed an application by kindergarten teacher M. Indira Gandhi to adduce fresh evidence in her lawsuit against the Inspector-General of Police (IGP) and three others over their alleged failure to locate her ex-husband, who absconded with their daughter. Justice Mohamed Zaini, who sat with Datuk Faizah Jamaludin and Datuk Mohd Radzi Abdul Hamid, said the evidence was not available during the High Court trial and only surfaced after the proceedings had concluded. “The evidence sought to be introduced relates to events that occurred well after the conclusion of the trial in the High Court and after the hearing of the appeal before this court.

items.title: Kota Tinggi shooting: Gunman was not drunk, cops say
items.summary: Police are now looking into the number of shots fired as investigations into the suspect's motive continue. District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the magistrate's court here has granted a seven-day remand order for the main suspect. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad confirmed that one of the victims, a Vietnamese woman, owed money to the suspect.

items.title: Jasin MP, Harakah apology to minister Fadhlina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Jasin member of parliament Zulkifli Ismail and Harakah newspaper have issued a public apology to Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek over defamatory statements made in 2023. Reading out the agreed judgment in the Sessions Court today, Zulkifli said the defendants acknowledged that the statements had led to misinterpretation and public misunderstanding, damaging the minister's reputation. "We therefore agree to fully, unconditionally and unequivocally withdraw the statements," he said.

items.title: Man who went viral for peeping on woman at Sentul LRT toilet fined RM3,500
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A food shop assistant who went viral after peeping on a woman while she was in a toilet cubicle at the Sentul LRT station was fined RM3,500 by the Magistrate’s Court here today. Magistrate Aina Azahra Arifin meted out the fine, in default four months in jail, on Abdul Samad Rosman, 23, after he pleaded guilty to insulting the modesty of the 44-year-old woman at the women’s toilet in Bandar Baru Sentul at 8 am on April 16. Deputy Public Prosecutor Shafika Azwa Fikri urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence as a deterrent, taking into account public safety and interest.

items.title: Health Ministry deploys medical and psychosocial teams to support 9,000 Sandakan fire victims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 20 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has mobilised frontline health teams to support victims of a fire in Sandakan, Sabah, including providing counselling services and essential medical care at temporary relief centres. “MOH has mobilised frontline personnel, including counsellors, nurses, medical officers and assistant medical officers, to provide the necessary assistance,” he said at a press conference after launching the National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS) 2025: Elderly Health findings here today. Dzulkefly said the teams deployed are also providing psychosocial support to victims, particularly those experiencing emotional distress following the fire.

items.title: Police rescue Chinese national held captive three days over friend’s RM100,000 gambling debt in KL Airbnb, two arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Police rescued a Chinese national who was allegedly held captive for three days at an Airbnb unit in Jalan Peel here after being used as a guarantor for a friend’s gambling debt. Cheras Police Chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud said two local men, both aged 30, were arrested during a 9pm raid on suspicion of confining the victim at the premises. “Initial investigations revealed that the 36-year-old victim was made a guarantor for a friend who owed RM100,000 in gambling debts.

items.title: Mara mulls measures to support entrepreneurs affected by global economic situation - Zahid
items.summary: DUNGUN: Mara is examining several support measures to assist entrepreneurs under its programmes who are affected by the global economic challenges due to the current geopolitical uncertainties. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said among those being examined is to reduce the rental rate of premises for affected entrepreneurs. Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said that apart from that, Mara will evaluate the implementation of rescheduling financing payments to give its entrepreneurs space to stabilise their financial position.

items.title: Terengganu Regent urges economic transformation to reduce dependence on Petroleum royalties
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Regent of Terengganu, Tengku Muhammad Ismail Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin, today called on the state government to formulate strategies to shift from exporting raw materials to developing downstream products, in line with the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 launched by the federal government. His Royal Highness said such structural economic transformation is crucial to reduce the state's dependence on volatile petroleum royalty income and to narrow development gaps between districts. Also present was Tengku Sri Setia Mahkota Raja Tengku Dato Seri Muhammad Mua'az.

items.title: Major 7.5-magnitude quake hits off Japan, tsunami warning issued
items.summary: TOKYO: An earthquake with a magnitude of 7.5 struck off the northeastern coast of Japan today, as authorities urged residents to stay away from coastal areas where tsunami waves of up to 3 metres (9.84 ft) were expected. The biggest waves were expected in Iwate, Aomori and Hokkaido prefectures, authorities said. There are no nuclear power plants currently in operation in Hokkaido and Tohoku regions but Hokkaido Electric Power Co and Tohoku Electric Power Co have a number of shutdown nuclear power plants there.

items.title: Displaced Lebanese hesitant to return home as lasting truce uncertain
items.summary: AFTER the ceasefire in Lebanon took hold, Samah Hajoul headed back to her apartment in south Beirut – but only long enough to grab fresh clothes, feeling safer in her tent as she wonders whether the truce will last. The ceasefire, which entered its second day Saturday, has granted many people the chance to check on their homes in areas where Hizbollah holds sway, like the southern suburbs, which were heavily bombed by Israel. "If the ceasefire is consolidated, we will return to our homes," she said.

items.title: Fahmi: MCMC logs 203,918 takedown requests since Jan 1, 91pc tied to gambling and scams
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that 91 per cent of the content requested to be removed was related to online gambling and scams. “Of that figure, 61 per cent falls under gambling, while scams account for 30 per cent. Also present were Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah, MCMC managing director Abdul Karim Fakir Ali and MCMC state affairs chief officer Bukhari Yahya.

items.title: Sister of cop allegedly killed by drink-driver demands heavy sentence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Naim Mullah Mohd Affandi had applied to join the police force three times before he was finally accepted. So I encouraged him to accept the opportunity," said his sister, Nur Lylatul Qadrina. Their grandparents, said Nur Lylatul, feel his absence so acutely that they cannot bring themselves to look at the clothes he left behind.

items.title: Fahmi: Second Malaysian oil tanker expected to arrive this weekend
items.summary: He said the shipment, following the arrival of the first vessel on April 17 carrying one million barrels of crude oil, would help strengthen Malaysia's supply security. Fahmi, who is also the Madani government spokesman, reiterated that the temporary adjustment of the Budi95 subsidy quota to 200 litres per month would remain in place until national fuel supply stabilises. Meanwhile, Fahmi said fuel supply across 639 petrol stations in Johor remains stable, with no disruptions or shortages reported.

items.title: False promise leads to life on the streets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amid the bustle of the capital, stories of hardship among the homeless continue to unfold, with many forced to seek shelter along five-foot ways and public spaces. Worse still, without family or acquaintances to rely on, they depend solely on public generosity and the kindness of strangers. He said he has been living on the streets for a week around Pasar Seni, with no friends or family in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Second Malaysian oil‑laden vessel clears Strait of Hormuz, due this weekend, says Fahmi
items.summary: He said the shipment, following the arrival of the first vessel on April 17 carrying one million litres of crude oil, would help strengthen Malaysia’s supply security. Fahmi, who is also the Madani Government spokesperson, reiterated that the temporary adjustment of the BUDI95 subsidy quota to 200 litres per month would remain in place until national fuel supply stabilises. Meanwhile, Fahmi said fuel supply across 639 petrol stations in Johor remains stable, with no disruptions or shortages reported.

items.title: SC, Bursa roll out MY Value Up to drive value creation among PLCs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission (SC) and Bursa Malaysia Bhd today launched the MY Value Up Programme to boost the valuation and market appeal of Malaysian public-listed companies (PLCs). Aligned with the Capital Market Masterplan 2026-2030, the initiative is a joint commitment by the SC and Bursa Malaysia to foster a more dynamic corporate landscape. SC chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said the new masterplan sets a bold vision for a more dynamic and competitive capital market.

items.title: Man shot in face during attempted cattle theft in Felda Kemahang 1
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: A settler's son was injured after he was shot by a group of men believed to be cattle smugglers in Felda Kemahang 1 today. In the 3am incident, the victim, in his 40s, was sleeping when a group of four to five men entered the village and attempted to steal cattle. "The suspects fired several shots, one of which struck the victim on the cheek."

items.title: Perak to form special committee to tackle telecom cable theft, sabotage
items.summary: IPOH: Perak will establish a special cross-agency committee to coordinate enforcement and response efforts against trespassing, sabotage and theft involving telecommunications infrastructure, including cable theft. State Communications, Multimedia and Non-Governmental Organisations (NGO) Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi said the committee would be chaired by him, with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) as secretariat, while telecommunications companies and enforcement agencies, including the police, would also be involved. Azlan said the provision was expected to be a stronger deterrent, as many cable theft cases had previously been prosecuted under general theft offences carrying lighter penalties.

items.title: Singapore GasCo secures extra LNG cargoes, eyes term deals despite war volatility
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore's state gas buyer GasCo has secured additional cargoes of liquefied natural gas to offset supply shortfalls triggered by the US-Israel war on Iran, and plans to seek long-term supply deals this year, despite market volatility. "While a portion of our LNG imports has been curtailed, Singapore GasCo has taken proactive steps to secure additional cargoes to augment existing supply sources," Chief Executive Alan Heng told Reuters in an emailed response. "In the short term, our focus is on securing LNG cargoes to make up for supply curtailed by existing suppliers."

items.title: Thunderstorms in KL, Putrajaya and eight states until 6pm - MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecasted to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, and eight states until 6pm today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said. The eight affected states are Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim), Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Bera and Rompin), Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Jempol and Tampin), Melaka (Melaka Tengah and Jasin), Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat and Pontian) and Sarawak (Kuching and Serian). In Sabah, the affected areas include the Interior (Kuala Penyu and Beaufort), the West Coast (Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud) and Kudat (Kota Marudu).

items.title: Severe weather alert: Thunderstorms in KL, Putrajaya and eight states until 6pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecasted to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, and eight states until 6pm today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said. The eight affected states are Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim), Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Bera and Rompin), Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Jempol and Tampin), Melaka (Melaka Tengah and Jasin), Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat and Pontian) and Sarawak (Kuching and Serian). Di Sabah, the affected areas include the Interior (Kuala Penyu and Beaufort), the West Coast (Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud) and Kudat (Kota Marudu).

items.title: Michigan refuses Trump administration demand for 2024 election ballots
items.summary: NEW YORK: Michigan officials on Sunday pushed back on a US Department of Justice demand for Detroit-area ballots and other materials related to the 2024 election, accusing the Trump administration of trying to cast doubt on the integrity of US elections. The Justice Department last week sent a letter demanding ballots, ballot receipts and ballot envelopes to the clerk in Wayne County, home to the heavily Democratic-leaning city of Detroit, according to Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel. Nessel's office released the DOJ's letter, authored by Assistant Attorney General Harmeet Dhillon, along with a reply vowing to fight the request.

items.title: UMT student who drowned attempting to save friend was dean's award winner
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Suraya Hassan, the Universiti Malaysia Terengganu student who drowned attempting to save her friend yesterday, was a top scorer who consistently received the dean's award. On Friday, she was up on stage receiving the award for the fifth consecutive time, choosing to wear a white baju kurung. "My daughter was determined to wear a white baju kurung when receiving her dean's award.

items.title: PM announces measures to ease cost pressures on micro, small and medium enterprises amid global energy crisis
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 20 – Prime Minister and Finance Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today continued his engagement session with micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) to gain direct insight into challenges on the ground and to formulate immediate steps to assist businesses affected by the global energy crisis, which is driving up operating costs and disrupting supply chains. Based on direct input from MSMEs, the government today announced several immediate measures to ease cost pressures and ensure business continuity. Under the Business Financing Guarantee Corporation (SJPP), a guarantee allocation of RM5 billion will be made available to support affected MSMEs, particularly in the construction, agriculture and agri-food, logistics and transport, as well as tourism sectors.

items.title: Batu Pahat fatal crash: 'I stopped receiving their location updates,' says grieving mother
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Yesterday, Norashikin Mohamad, 57, who was tailing her sons in a separate car, suddenly stopped receiving their live location updates. The crash, which occurred at 4.30pm along Jalan Kluang in Ayer Hitam, claimed the lives of Fazli Yuzaini Zainal Abidin, 30, and Irfan Helmi, 20. Their two other brothers — Azrul Nizam, 29, and Zul Fahmi, 26 — who were travelling with them, survived.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: 320 cases of illegal misuse of subsidies detected under Ops Tiris 4.0
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 20 — A total of 320 legal violations have been recorded under the integrated anti-leakage operation, dubbed Ops Tiris 4.0, since March 16, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said the cases were uncovered through 18,261 inspections conducted nationwide. Also present were Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali and his deputy Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh, as well as Sabah Assistant Minister in the Chief Minister’s Department Datuk Caesar Mandela Malakun.

items.title: Widow of drink-driving victim wants offender to provide for family for life
items.summary: Nor Nadia Abdul Majid said she could not accept what she described as lenient punishment for those responsible for deaths caused by driving under the influence of alcohol. 'Has Papa not called? she said.

items.title: Rumen Radev, ex-pilot who wants to give Bulgaria wings
items.summary: FORMER fighter pilot Rumen Radev has vowed to lead Bulgaria out of a chronic political crisis, with his grouping winning Sunday's general election – the eighth in five years. Many voters see Radev – who was Bulgaria's president until earlier this year – as the only person capable of giving the corruption-plagued Balkan nation a fresh start. That should be used," Radev, who was president for nine years, said recently.

items.title: PM Anwar: Government committed to uplifting dignity, welfare of Kelantan people
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — The Madani Government remains focused on continuously uplifting the dignity and welfare of the people of Kelantan comprehensively, covering transport, education, development, and the strengthening of religious institutions. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said these efforts are being driven with clear determination to ensure that no one is left behind and that every segment of society can enjoy fair and equitable prosperity and well-being. Anwar, who conducted a working visit to Kelantan last Saturday, officially opened the new terminal of the Sultan Ismail Petra Airport and attended the Pasir Puteh district development meeting.

items.title: Azalina urges caution on Federal Court appointments ahead of Istana Negara ceremony
items.summary: Her remarks come amid reports that Court of Appeal judges Datuk Ravinthran Paramaguru and Datuk Azmi Ariffin are set to be elevated to the Federal Court. [[nid:1422089]] Ravinthran was appointed judicial commissioner in 2009 before becoming a High Court judge in 2013 and later a Court of Appeal judge in 2019. Azmi was appointed judicial commissioner in 2014, elevated to the High Court in 2017, and promoted to the Court of Appeal in June 2023. earlier , it was reported that six High Court judges – Dean Wayne Daly, Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, Datuk Amarjeet Singh Serjit Singh, Datuk Seri Mohd Radzi Harun, Datuk Seri Latifah Tahar and Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Mohd Salleh – are slated to be elevated to the Court of Appeal.

items.title: Selangor Sultan orders modest Silver Jubilee celebration amid economic concerns
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 20 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has decreed that celebrations marking the 25th anniversary of his reign be held modestly, with several planned programmes scaled down or cancelled. His Royal Highness said the decision was made after consulting the Selangor State Legislative Assembly, taking into account the current uncertain global economic climate and its impact on the people’s well-being. Sultan Sharafuddin said this in his royal address at the opening of the Fourth Session of the 15th Selangor State Legislative Assembly today.

items.title: Man in his 70s remanded over fatal triple shooting in Kota Tinggi
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 20 — A 71-year-old man suspected of involvement in a shooting that left three people dead in Taman Kota Jaya yesterday has been remanded for seven days until April 26. The senior citizen arrived at the court complex at about 9 am, handcuffed and escorted by police personnel from the Kota Tinggi district police headquarters. Yesterday, Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said two local men and a foreign woman, aged between 37 and 63, were found dead after being shot at a restaurant in Taman Kota Jaya.

items.title: Sabah weighing redevelopment plans, including land reclamation, after Sandakan fire
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 20 — The state government is looking at several proposal on how or whether to rebuild Kampung Bahagia in Batu Sapi after a major fire which destroyed almost 1,000 houses early yesterday morning. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that several proposals have been made including one for land reclamation to support future housing development. “We have several plans, but I believe it will take one to two weeks before we receive a detailed report on the total number of fire victims and how to proceed.

items.title: Batik Air rolls out up to 35pc fare discounts as travellers seek flexible bookings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Batik Air has introduced discounts of up to 35 per cent on selected economy fares, as airlines respond to growing demand for more flexible travel options. In a statement, the airline said the offer covers its Economy Value and Economy Flexi fares, available for booking until April 30 for travel through January 31, 2027. Both fare types include complimentary date changes, with Economy Value allowing a one-time change and Economy Flexi offering unlimited changes, subject to fare differences.

items.title: KLIA baggage system glitch traced to power fault, 1,061 bags shortshipped, says Transport Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A disruption to Kuala Lumpur International Airport’s (KLIA) baggage handling system on April 18 was triggered by a high-voltage power fault at a Bukit Raja substation, the Transport Ministry said today. In a statement, the ministry said preliminary findings showed a 132kV Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) trip at about 4.54pm caused a voltage dip that disrupted operations at Terminal 1, leading to a large-scale system failure affecting baggage handling for both arriving and departing flights. Although the system was supported by uninterruptible power supply (UPS) units, six UPS units were also affected, exposing weaknesses in system resilience, it added.

items.title: State extends assistance to 2,001 fire victims in Sandakan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 20 — Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor visited fire victims at Kampung Bahagia here and the temporary evacuation centre at Taman Batu Sapi PPR. He also handed over assistance to those affected, including contributions from the State Government, Sabah Islamic Religious Council (Muis), Sabah Baitulmal Corporation and others. He said the assistance was hoped to help ease the victims’ burden, with 2,001 affected individuals, including non-citizens, to receive related support.

items.title: PADU to offer data analytics services to government agencies via portal
items.summary: According to a media statement issued by the ministry on April 20, 2026, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said starting May 2026, the ministry will begin offering PADU data analytics services, known as Analytic-as-a-Service, to government agencies through the PADU analytics portal. The basis of PADU’s development is the integration of data from various agencies with information updated by the public. The inclusiveness and availability of data allow PADU to provide Profile-as-a-Service, Analytics-as-a-Service, Data-as-a-Service, and a Unified Data Platform for government agencies to utilise.

items.title: Health Ministry: Study shows just 14pc of Malaysians over 60 ‘ageing well’
items.summary: Under the mental health domain, the prevalence of depression were found to have dropped to 8 per cent compared to 11.2 per cent in 2018, while severe depression declined to 2.2 per cent from 5.3 per cent. Still, cases of dementia among persons 60 older were found to have increased, from 8.5 per cent in 2018 to 9.8 per cent, or nearly one out of ten. Hypertension is also at a staggering 73.1 per cent while those reported with high bad cholestrol, 76 per cent.

items.title: Sultan of Selangor reprimands state government for lack of seriousness in flood mitigation efforts, despite his past counsel
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 20 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has rebuked the state government for failing to resolve persistent flooding issues despite repeated advisories. According to The Star , the Ruler delivered the reprimand during his royal address at the opening of the Selangor state legislative assembly today. Sultan Sharafuddin noted that he had previously suggested the state seek expertise from overseas, particularly the Netherlands, to find effective solutions for Selangor’s flood problems.

items.title: ‘I got scammed too’: Johari Abdul Ghani exposes fake Miti letter promising RM80,000 investment returns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani today revealed how his name and the ministry were recently exploited in a scam targeting the public. In a Facebook reel today, Johari said he was approached during an open house by an individual who presented him with a letter bearing a Miti letterhead and asked him to verify its authenticity. “In truth, Miti has never issued such a letter.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan’s unique ruler selection system: Who are the Undang Yang Empat and what role are they playing?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A rare constitutional and adat dispute in Negeri Sembilan has thrust the state’s unique traditional system into the national spotlight. What are the Undang? How are Undang selected?

items.title: China deal helps Malaysia avoid medical device shortages, says Health Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — The supply of medical devices in Malaysia remains stable and under control despite the global energy crisis affecting the availability of certain high-risk imported products. As part of proactive efforts, the Medical Device Authority (MDA) has finalised a strategic agreement with Chinese authorities to increase the supply of resin and naphtha for local industrial use. “This initiative aims to stabilise the supply chain and ensure the continued production of critical medical devices in Malaysia.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s ‘active non-alignment’ helps cut red tape in global defence supply chains amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — Malaysia’s pursuit of active non-alignment enables it to maintain working relationships with major powers even during global uncertainties, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Speaking at this year’s Defence Services Asia and National Security Asia conference, Anwar said the policy allows Malaysian suppliers to easily penetrate global supply chains without encountering political complications. Anwar said Malaysian companies manufacture high-quality wing structures and precision components, with one local enterprise holding a Tier 1 supplier status with a leading global aero-engine manufacturer.

items.title: Boat overturns in Kota Belud with eight onboard; six rescued, one dead, search continues for one missing
items.summary: KOTA BELUD, April 20 — A boat carrying eight people capsized off Kampung Tamau Batu Gadung here yesterday afternoon, leaving one dead, one missing, and six survivors. The 18-year-old victim was found by members of the public at about 9.50pm, roughly 700 metres from the capsized boat location, before being taken to hospital by villagers, Malay news outlet BH reported. The 18-year-old was later confirmed dead at Kota Belud Hospital.

items.title: Cross-border cattle run for RM70 each ends with duo in handcuffs in Pasir Mas
items.summary: PASIR MAS, April 20 — A late-night patrol in Kelantan ended with more than just traffic checks after authorities uncovered a lorry packed with 13 cows being moved without proper paperwork. Two men aged 22 and 25 were arrested on the spot in Kampung Manan after General Operations Force (GOF) officers stopped the vehicle during Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 9.30pm yesterday, New Straits Times reported. He said the lorry and cattle were seized immediately.

items.title: Ex-Undang Luak Sungai Ujong’s move to depose Negeri Sembilan Ruler ‘disloyal’, should face legal action, says official
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 20 — A former Undang Luak Sungai Ujong’s move to sign and read a declaration purporting to depose the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, has been condemned as an act of disloyalty and without any authority. Telaga Undang official Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri said Datuk Mubarak Dohak’s declaration was carried out without any customary or constitutional mandate, Malay news outlet BH reported today. “The Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu also never gave him a mandate to do so.

items.title: Sarawak eyes hydrogen as diesel prices remain volatile
items.summary: KUCHING, April 20 — Sarawak is strengthening its push towards alternative energy sources, particularly hydrogen, in response to ongoing volatility in global diesel prices linked to geopolitical tensions and market dynamics. According to Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg, hydrogen production is expected to become relatively more cost-competitive than diesel, supported by advances in electrolysis technology, including improvements in membrane efficiency. “With today’s diesel prices, the production of hydrogen will be cheaper than diesel; the electrolysis process will be cheaper because of the new membrane in the technology.

items.title: ‘We thought they were fireworks’: Kota Tinggi traders recount chaos before three killed in shooting
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 20 — Panic gripped a business area in Taman Kota Jaya here yesterday after witnesses reported hearing a loud explosion that was initially thought to be firecrackers, but was later found to be a shooting that left three people dead. He said the suspect then went to his vehicle before returning to the scene. Everyone was panicked, so we immediately closed shop,” he said.
